quiz.title: Unit 2 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Isotopes and Atomic Notation
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Isotopes, Atomic Number, Mass Number, Nuclear Symbol Notation, Hydrogen Isotopes, Neutral Atom Relationships

questions.question: Which of the following describes isotopes?
options: Same atomic number, different mass number
Same mass number, different atomic number
Same number of electrons, different protons
Same number of neutrons, different electrons
questions.explanation: Isotopes are atoms of the same element (same atomic number/protons) that have different numbers of neutrons, resulting in different mass numbers.

questions.question: Chlorine has two naturally occurring isotopes: Cl-35 and Cl-37. Which subatomic particle differs between them?
questions.explanation: Isotopes have the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons. Cl-35 has 18 neutrons while Cl-37 has 20 neutrons (both have 17 protons).

questions.question: Which statement is correct for a neutral atom?
options: Number of protons = number of electrons
Number of protons = number of neutrons
Number of neutrons = number of electrons
questions.explanation: In a neutral atom, the positive charge from protons equals the negative charge from electrons, so the number of protons equals the number of electrons.

questions.question: If an atom has 26 protons and 30 neutrons, its atomic number is:
questions.explanation: The atomic number is defined as the number of protons. With 26 protons, the atomic number is 26 (this is iron, Fe).

questions.question: The symbol $^{23}_{11}Na$ represents an atom with:
questions.explanation: In nuclear notation $^{A}_{Z}X$, Z is the atomic number (protons) and A is the mass number (protons + neutrons). So $^{23}_{11}Na$ has 11 protons and $23 - 11 = 12$ neutrons.

questions.question: An atom has 15 protons, 16 neutrons, and 15 electrons. Its symbol is:
questions.explanation: With 15 protons, the atomic number is 15 (phosphorus). Mass number = protons + neutrons = 15 + 16 = 31. The correct notation is $^{31}_{15}P$.

questions.question: Which isotope notation represents 12 protons and 14 neutrons?
questions.explanation: 12 protons means atomic number 12 (magnesium, Mg). Mass number = protons + neutrons = 12 + 14 = 26. The notation is $^{26}_{12}Mg$.

questions.question: Which isotope of hydrogen has 1 proton and 2 neutrons?
questions.explanation: Tritium ($^{3}H$ or hydrogen-3) has 1 proton and 2 neutrons. Protium has 0 neutrons, and deuterium has 1 neutron.

questions.question: Which statement about isotopes is correct?
options: They have the same mass number
They have the same atomic number
They have the same number of neutrons
questions.explanation: Isotopes are atoms of the same element that have the same atomic number (same number of protons) but different mass numbers due to different numbers of neutrons.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about isotopes?
questions.explanation: Isotopes have identical chemical properties because they have the same electron configuration (same number of protons and electrons). However, they may have slightly different physical properties (like mass, density, or reaction rates) due to their different masses.

questions.question: Which of these carbon isotopes has the greatest mass?
questions.explanation: Carbon-14 has 6 protons and 8 neutrons (mass number 14), making it heavier than Carbon-12 (6 protons, 6 neutrons) and Carbon-13 (6 protons, 7 neutrons).

questions.question: Which isotope of hydrogen has no neutrons?
questions.explanation: Protium (¹H or hydrogen-1) is the most common hydrogen isotope with 1 proton and 0 neutrons. Deuterium (hydrogen-2) has 1 neutron, and tritium (hydrogen-3) has 2 neutrons.

questions.question: Which of these isotopes of Carbon is not naturally occurring?
questions.explanation: C-15 is not a naturally occurring isotope of carbon. It is a synthetic, radioactive isotope produced in laboratories with a very short half-life. C-12 and C-13 are stable and naturally abundant, while C-14 occurs in trace amounts naturally from cosmic ray interactions.

questions.question: Rutherford carried out experiments in which a beam of alpha particles was directed at a thin piece of metal foil. From these experiments he concluded that:
options: the positively charged parts of atoms are moving about with a velocity approaching the speed of light.
the positively charged parts of atoms are extremely small and extremely heavy particles.
the diameter of an electron is approximately equal to that of the nucleus.
electrons travel in circular orbits around the nucleus.
questions.explanation: Rutherford concluded that atoms have a small, dense, positively charged nucleus. The alpha particle deflection patterns showed that the positive charge (protons) is concentrated in an extremely small region (the nucleus) that is also extremely dense/heavy, containing most of the atom's mass.

questions.question: Which isotope of hydrogen has 1 proton and 1 neutron?
questions.explanation: Deuterium ($^{2}H$ or hydrogen-2) has 1 proton and 1 neutron. Protium has 0 neutrons, and tritium has 2 neutrons.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered isotopes and nuclear notation! ⚛️📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the relationship between protons, neutrons, and mass number.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on reading nuclear symbols and calculating neutrons.
scoreMessages.0: Please review isotopes, atomic number, mass number, and how to interpret nuclear notation.
